Professor View 01 / 27
Travel English -- 48 Aulas

Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita

Customer Success & Suporte Técnico Internacional

B1 (Intermediário) São Paulo, SP Customer Success Manager — Salesforce 60 min · Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Nome

Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita

Idade

40 anos

Cidade

São Paulo, SP

Profissão

Customer Success Manager — Salesforce (clientes premium, Bradesco)

Nível

B1 (Intermediário) — uso diário funcional, busca correção e confiança

Foco

Business English: Customer Success & suporte técnico internacional, reuniões com times globais, escalações, correção gramatical

Frequência

Semanal — Online (Zoom)

Total de Aulas

20 aulas (60 min)

Personagem-Jornada

De: Uma gestora experiente que usa inglês todos os dias com times globais (Índia, EUA), mas que percebe os próprios deslizes gramaticais em tempo real — tropeca no Present Perfect, hesita nos tempos verbais e sente que isso mina sua autoridade em reuniões críticas e escalações.

Para: Uma Customer Success Manager que conduz calls com times de produto e suporte internacionais com correção e confiança, domina o Present Perfect e os tempos verbais que mais usa, entende sotaques não nativos sem esforço e fecha QBRs com clientes premium falando com a mesma autoridade que tem em português. Em 20 aulas.

Promessa Transformadora

De "errar a gramática que eu mesma percebo" a conduzir reuniões globais com correção e segurança. O desafio da Patrícia não é vocabulário — ela já vive em inglês no trabalho. É precisão gramatical sob pressão: usar o tempo verbal certo na hora certa, sem hesitar. Cada aula trabalha a estrutura que ela mais usa em cenários reais de Customer Success, com correção focada e prática oral intensa.

Forças
  • Imersão funcional diária: usa inglês no trabalho todos os dias
  • Vocabulário de CRM/Customer Success/Salesforce já ativo
  • Alta autoconsciência: identifica os próprios erros em tempo real
  • Forte motivação profissional (reuniões globais semanais, escalações)
  • Base de Berlitz intensivo e prática contInua no Preply
Pontos de Melhoria
  • Present Perfect e contraste entre tempos verbais (fraqueza declarada)
  • Confiança abalada ao perceber o próprio erro durante a fala
  • Listening para sotaques não nativos (indiano, americano)
  • Precisão sob pressão em escalações e reuniões críticas
  • Estruturas diplomáticas para dizer "não" e gerenciar expectativas

Currículo Completo — 20 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalStatus
1Introducing Yourself & Your RolePresent simple vs present continuousPrimeira call com um colega globalConcluída
2Talking About Your ExperiencePresent Perfect vs Past SimpleFalar da trajetória profissionalAtual
3Status Updates & Ongoing WorkPresent continuous + currently / at the momentDar um status update numa reuniãoPróxima
4Asking Effective Questions on CallsQuestion forms + indirect questionsFazer perguntas claras e educadasPróxima
5Review — A Customer Check-in CallConsolidação do Bloco 1Call de check-in simuladaPróxima
6Onboarding & Driving AdoptionSequencing (first / then / once / after that)Explicar um plano de onboardingPróxima
7Understanding Customer NeedsModais de sugestão (should / could / I'd recommend)Levantar e recomendar soluçõesPróxima
8Handling a Technical IssuePresent Perfect for results (we've fixed...)Reportar a resolução de um problemaPróxima
9Escalations Under PressureFirst conditional + linguagem de urgênciaConduzir uma escalação críticaPróxima
10Review — Resolving a Critical EscalationConsolidação do Bloco 2Escalação simuladaPróxima
11Working Across Cultures (India / US)Polite directness + listening a sotaquesAlinhar com times de Índia e EUAPróxima
12Product Calls & RoadmapFuture forms (will / going to / present continuous)Discutir roadmap e releasesPróxima
13Giving & Receiving FeedbackDiplomatic softening (perhaps / it might be...)Dar e receber feedback construtivoPróxima
14Follow-ups & Action ItemsReported speech (said / told + backshift)Recapitular e atribuir action itemsPróxima
15Review — A Global Product SyncConsolidação do Bloco 3Sync de produto simuladoPróxima
16Presenting Customer Health & MetricsComparativos + linguagem de tendênciaApresentar métricas de saúde da contaPróxima
17Negotiating & Managing ExpectationsSecond conditional + modais diplomáticosNegociar e alinhar expectativasPróxima
18Difficult Conversations & Saying No PolitelyHedging + I'm afraid / unfortunatelyRecusar e reassegurar com diplomaciaPróxima
19Renewals & Driving ValuePresent Perfect for results + persuasãoConduzir uma conversa de renovaçãoPróxima
20Capstone — Quarterly Business Review (QBR)Integração de todas as estruturasQBR completo simuladoPróxima

Metodologia

1. Precisão Gramatical Focada

A Patrícia já se comunica; o objetivo é correção. Cada aula isola a estrutura que ela mais usa (Present Perfect, tempos verbais) e a treina até virar automática, com correção cirúrgica.

2. Cenários Reais de Customer Success

Todo conteúdo usa o mundo dela: calls com times globais, onboarding de clientes premium, escalações críticas, roadmap de produto, QBRs. Ela pratica o que vai fazer de verdade.

3. Confiança Sob Pressão

Role-plays graduados (guided → semi-free → free) recriam a pressão das reuniões reais, para que a correção resista mesmo quando ela está sob estresse.

4. Listening para Sotaques Globais

Os listenings usam vozes variadas (americana, e contextos de Índia/EUA) para treinar a compreensão de sotaques não nativos que ela enfrenta toda semana.

5. Erro como Diagnóstico

A Patrícia percebe os próprios erros — usamos isso a favor: feedback adiado, análise dos próprios deslizes e foco nos padrões recorrentes, transformando autocrítica em progresso.

Mapa de Personalidade

IntrovertidaExtrovertida
Aprendiz leitura/escritaAprendiz auditiva
ImpulsivaReflexiva
Tolerância ao erro baixaTolerância ao erro alta
Energia caóticaEnergia calma e estruturada
Insegura em inglêsConfiante em inglês
Foco acadêmicoFoco profissional
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Introducing Yourself & Your Role

A first call with a new global colleague. Key words: Customer Success Manager, adoption, stakeholder, account, onboarding, rollout, premium client, point of contact, deployment, scope. Structures: present simple (routine duties) vs present continuous (current projects).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Customer Success Manager -- the person who helps clients get full value from a product (gerente de sucesso do cliente)
"I am a Customer Success Manager at Salesforce."
Adoption -- how much customers actually use a product (adoção, uso real)
"My job is to drive adoption across the account."
Stakeholder -- a person with an interest in a project's outcome (parte interessada)
"I align every stakeholder before a rollout."
Account -- a company you manage as a client (conta, cliente)
"Bradesco is my largest account."
Onboarding -- helping a new customer start using a product (integração inicial)
"We are in the onboarding phase right now."
Rollout -- the gradual release of a feature to users (lançamento gradual)
"The rollout starts next month."
Premium client -- a top, high-value customer (cliente premium)
"I support our premium clients in Brazil."
Point of contact -- the main person people reach out to (ponto de contato)
"I am your point of contact for this project."
Deployment -- putting software into active use (implantação)
"The deployment is almost ready."
Scope -- the amount and limits of work in a project (escopo)
"Let us align on the scope of the call."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Adoption
Stakeholder
Account
Onboarding
Rollout
Premium client
Point of contact
Deployment
Scope

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Patrícia is a Customer Success Manager at Salesforce. Every week, she manages twelve premium accounts and runs calls with global teams. "I am responsible for driving adoption," she says. Right now, she is leading the onboarding for Bradesco, her biggest account. Her team is rolling out a new dashboard this quarter, and the deployment is almost ready. On the first call, a new colleague asks: "What do you do? What are you working on right now?" Patrícia answers with confidence, because she knows the difference between her routine duties and her current project.

1. Por que dizemos "she manages" e "she runs" com -s no final?
A Porque a ação aconteceu no passado.
B Porque, no present simple, he/she/it leva -s no verbo.
C Porque "Patrícia" é plural.
2. "Right now, she is leading the onboarding" usa o present continuous para expressar:
A Um projeto que está acontecendo agora (não uma rotina).
B Uma ação que ela faz toda semana, sempre.
C Algo que terminou ontem.
3. Which sentence about a routine duty is correct?
A "Every week, I am running calls with global teams."
B "Every week, I run calls with global teams."
C "Every week, I running calls with global teams."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Simple vs Present Continuous

GRAMMAR

Quando usar cada tempo para falar do seu trabalho (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Present Simple
I / you / we + verb		Rotinas e responsabilidades fixas. Routine duties and permanent facts.I manage twelve accounts.
he / she / it + verb+s3ª pessoa SEMPRE leva -s. Third person always adds -s.She leads the rollout.
Present Continuous
am / is / are + verb-ing		Projetos de agora, ações temporárias. Current projects, happening now.I am leading the onboarding.
responsible for + verb-ingSempre "for" + verbo com -ing. Never "responsible to".I am responsible for managing adoption.
Signal wordsevery day, usually → simple · now, currently, this week → continuous

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Every week, I calls with global teams."
"Right now, I the Bradesco onboarding."
"David in Austin."
"This quarter, we a new dashboard."
"I am responsible driving adoption."
"Let us align on the of the project."

Stage 2: Put the Introduction in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma apresentação numa primeira call na ordem correta.

?Say what you are responsible for.
?Suggest a next step or a follow-up call.
?Greet the colleague and say "Nice to meet you."
?Describe your current project (present continuous).
?Say your name and your role.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Hi, I am Patrícia. I am a Customer Success Manager at Salesforce.
Oi, eu sou a Patrícia. Sou gerente de sucesso do cliente na Salesforce.
I am responsible for driving adoption across my accounts.
Sou responsável por aumentar a adoção nas minhas contas.
Right now, I am leading the onboarding for our biggest account.
Agora, estou liderando a integração da nossa maior conta.
We are rolling out a new dashboard this quarter.
Estamos lançando um novo painel neste trimestre.
You are my main point of contact for any scope questions.
Você é meu principal ponto de contato para qualquer dúvida de escopo.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A new colleague asks: "What do you do?" You answer:
A "I do customer things."
B "I am a Customer Success Manager. I help premium clients get value from Salesforce."
C "I am responsible to the clients."
He asks: "What are you working on right now?" The best answer is:
A "I work on the Bradesco onboarding right now."
B "I am currently leading the Bradesco onboarding."
C "I am lead the Bradesco onboarding."
You want to describe a fixed responsibility. Which sentence is correct?
A "I am responsible for managing twelve accounts."
B "I am responsible to manage twelve accounts."
C "I responsible for manage twelve accounts."
You want to end the first call and keep the contact. You say:
A "It was great meeting you. Let us schedule a follow-up call this week."
B "Okay, bye, finish now."
C "We are schedule a call since this week."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are on a first call with a new global colleague. Introduce yourself: your name, your role, what you are responsible for (present simple), and the project you are leading right now (present continuous). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1Hi, I am Patrícia. Nice to meet you.Oi, sou a Patrícia. Prazer.
2I am a Customer Success Manager at Salesforce.Sou gerente de sucesso do cliente na Salesforce.
3I am responsible for driving adoption.Sou responsável por aumentar a adoção.
4Right now, I am leading the Bradesco rollout.Agora, estou liderando o rollout do Bradesco.
5Let us schedule a follow-up call this week.Vamos marcar uma call de acompanhamento esta semana.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

Talking About Your Experience

Sharing your career and background with a new colleague. Key words: experience, background, achievement, career, lead, handle, responsibility, previously, recently, so far. Structure: Present Perfect vs Past Simple.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Experience -- the knowledge and skill you get from doing a job (experiência)
"I have a lot of experience with global teams."
Background -- your education and past work history (formação, histórico)
"Tell me a bit about your background."
Achievement -- something important you succeeded in doing (conquista)
"Closing that deal was my biggest achievement."
Career -- your working life and the jobs you have over time (carreira)
"I started my career in technical support."
Lead -- to be in charge of a team or a project (liderar)
"I lead the onboarding for our biggest account."
Handle -- to deal with a difficult situation or problem (lidar com)
"I handle our most demanding clients."
Responsibility -- a duty you are in charge of (responsabilidade)
"Adoption is my main responsibility."
Previously -- before now, in an earlier time (anteriormente)
"Previously, I worked at a fintech company."
Recently -- a short time ago, not long ago (recentemente)
"I recently joined the customer experience group."
So far -- up to now, until this moment (até agora)
"So far, I have led three rollouts."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Experience
Background
Achievement
Career
Lead
Handle
Responsibility
Previously
Recently
So far

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Patrícia has worked in customer success for eight years. She started her career in technical support, and in 2015 she moved to a fintech company. There, she handled the largest clients. In 2019, she joined Salesforce. Since then, she has led three major rollouts and has supported more than thirty premium accounts. "So far, I have never missed a renewal," she says proudly. When a new colleague asked about her background last week, she told her whole story with confidence.

1. Por que "she joined Salesforce in 2019" usa o past simple?
A Porque a ação ainda continua hoje.
B Porque há um tempo terminado e específico (in 2019).
C Porque é uma rotina que se repete toda semana.
2. "Since then, she has led three rollouts" usa o present perfect porque:
A O período começou no passado e continua até agora.
B A ação terminou num dia específico do passado.
C É algo que ela vai fazer no futuro.
3. Which sentence about experience up to now is correct?
A "I work here since 2019."
B "I have worked here since 2019."
C "I am working here since 2019."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect vs Past Simple

GRAMMAR

Quando usar cada tempo para falar da sua experiência (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Past Simple
verb + ed / irregular		Ação terminada com tempo definido. Finished action with a clear past time.I joined in 2019.
Present Perfect
have / has + past participle		Experiência até agora, tempo não terminado. Experience up to now.I have worked here since 2019.
since vs forsince + ponto no tempo · for + duração. Point in time vs length of time.since 2019 · for eight years
Irregular participlesParticípios irregulares comuns. Common irregular participles.lead → led · be → been · do → done
Signal wordsin 2019, last year, ago → past simple · for, since, so far, recently, ever, never → present perfect

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"I here since 2019."
"I Salesforce in 2019."
"Previously, I at a fintech company."
"So far, she three rollouts."
"I have many difficult clients."
"Tell me a bit about your ."

Stage 2: Tell Your Story in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de contar sua trajetória numa call na ordem correta.

?Mention one achievement so far (present perfect).
?Greet and say your current role.
?Ask the other person about their background.
?Say how long you have worked in your field (present perfect).
?Say what you did before, in a past job (past simple).

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

I have worked in customer success for eight years.
Trabalho em sucesso do cliente há oito anos.
I joined Salesforce in 2019.
Entrei na Salesforce em 2019.
Previously, I worked at a fintech company.
Anteriormente, trabalhei numa empresa de fintech.
So far, I have led three major rollouts.
Até agora, liderei três grandes lançamentos.
What is your biggest achievement so far?
Qual é a sua maior conquista até agora?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A colleague asks: "How long have you been in customer success?" You answer:
A "I am in customer success since eight years."
B "I have worked in customer success for eight years."
C "I work in customer success since eight years."
You want to give a dated fact about when you started. Which is correct?
A "I have joined Salesforce in 2019."
B "I joined Salesforce in 2019."
C "I join Salesforce in 2019."
You describe a job you had before this one. The best answer is:
A "Previously, I worked as an account manager."
B "Previously, I have worked as an account manager."
C "Previously, I am working as an account manager."
You want to talk about your experience up to now, using "so far". You say:
A "So far, I have led three major rollouts."
B "So far, I led three major rollouts last year."
C "So far, I am leading three major rollouts in 2019."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a new global colleague asks about your career. Tell your story: where you started, what you did before Salesforce (past simple), and what you have achieved so far (present perfect). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1I have worked in customer success for eight years.Trabalho em sucesso do cliente há oito anos.
2I joined Salesforce in 2019.Entrei na Salesforce em 2019.
3Previously, I worked at a fintech company.Anteriormente, trabalhei numa empresa de fintech.
4So far, I have led three major rollouts.Até agora, liderei três grandes lançamentos.
5Tell me a bit about your background.Conte-me um pouco sobre sua formação.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

Status Updates & Ongoing Work

Giving a clear project update on a call. Key words: update, progress, on track, milestone, blocker, pending, ongoing, priority, follow up, timeline. Structure: Present Continuous vs Present Simple.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Update -- the latest news about a project's status (atualização)
"Let me give you a quick update on the project."
Progress -- movement forward toward finishing something (progresso)
"We are making good progress this week."
On track -- going as planned, on schedule (no rumo certo)
"The rollout is on track for Friday."
Milestone -- an important stage you reach in a project (marco)
"We have reached an important milestone."
Blocker -- something that stops the work from moving forward (impedimento)
"There is one blocker we need to solve."
Pending -- waiting to be done or decided (pendente)
"The approval is still pending."
Ongoing -- still happening, not finished yet (em andamento)
"The migration is ongoing right now."
Priority -- the most important task to do first (prioridade)
"User training is our top priority."
Follow up -- to check or act on something after a meeting (dar seguimento)
"I will follow up with you tomorrow."
Timeline -- the planned schedule of dates for a project (cronograma)
"We are still on the timeline we agreed."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Update
Progress
On track
Milestone
Blocker
Pending
Ongoing
Priority
Follow up
Timeline

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

It is Monday morning, and Patrícia is preparing her weekly status update. Right now, the team is migrating the client's data, and the rollout is on track. Every Friday, she sends a short update to her manager. At the moment, there is one pending item: they are waiting for a security approval. "Our priority this week is user training," she writes. She is preparing the sessions now, and she will follow up on Friday. The project is ongoing, but the team is making good progress.

1. Por que "the team is migrating the data" usa o present continuous?
A Porque é uma rotina que se repete toda semana.
B Porque a ação está acontecendo agora, neste momento.
C Porque a ação terminou num dia específico do passado.
2. "Every Friday, she sends a short update" usa o present simple porque:
A É uma rotina, algo que se repete regularmente.
B É algo que está acontecendo só agora.
C É algo que já terminou ontem.
3. Which sentence about something happening now is correct?
A "At the moment, we test the connection."
B "At the moment, we are testing the connection."
C "At the moment, we are test the connection."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Continuous vs Present Simple

GRAMMAR

Quando usar cada tempo para dar um status update (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Present Continuous
am / is / are + verb-ing		Ação acontecendo agora ou nestes dias. An action happening now.I am updating the report now.
Present Simple
verb / verb-s		Rotina, hábito ou fato. Routine, habit or fact.I send a report every Friday.
Spelling -ingwork → working · prepare → preparing (tira o -e) · run → running (dobra a consoante).testing · migrating
ContractionsNa fala, encurtamos. We shorten it when speaking.I am = I'm · we are = we're · it is = it's
Signal wordsnow, right now, at the moment, currently, this week → continuous · usually, every day, always, often → simple

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Right now, I the status update."
"Every Friday, I a short update."
"At the moment, the team the data."
"The approval is still ."
"We for the approval."

Stage 2: Give the Update in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de um status update numa call na ordem correta.

?Mention any blocker or pending item.
?Greet and say which project you are updating.
?State your priority and when you will follow up.
?Say what is happening right now (present continuous).
?Say if the project is on track or behind.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Right now, we are migrating their data.
Agora, estamos migrando os dados deles.
Everything is on track for Friday.
Está tudo no rumo certo para sexta.
There is one pending item on my list.
Há um item pendente na minha lista.
Our priority this week is user training.
Nossa prioridade esta semana é o treinamento dos usuários.
I will follow up with you tomorrow.
Vou dar um retorno a você amanhã.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

Your manager asks: "What are you working on right now?" You answer:
A "Right now, I am preparing the training sessions."
B "Right now, I prepare the training sessions."
C "Right now, I am prepare the training sessions."
You describe a routine you do every week. Which is correct?
A "I am sending a status update every Friday."
B "I send a status update every Friday."
C "I sending a status update every Friday."
You want to report a problem that stops the work. The best answer is:
A "We have one blocker: the data is not ready yet."
B "We have one milestone: the data is not ready yet."
C "We have one timeline: the data is not ready yet."
You want to say the project is going as planned. You say:
A "The rollout is on track for Friday."
B "The rollout is pending for Friday."
C "The rollout is a blocker for Friday."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine your manager asks for a quick update on a real project. Give a status update: what is ongoing right now (present continuous), what is still pending, and what your priority is this week. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1Let me give you a quick update.Deixe-me dar uma atualização rápida.
2Right now, we are migrating their data.Agora, estamos migrando os dados deles.
3Everything is on track for Friday.Está tudo no rumo certo para sexta.
4There is one pending item on my list.Há um item pendente na minha lista.
5I will follow up with you tomorrow.Vou dar um retorno a você amanhã.
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Asking Effective Questions on Calls

Asking clear, polite questions to get the answers you need on a call. Key words: clarify, confirm, double-check, rephrase, agenda, raise, address, concern, recap, detail. Structure: question forms & indirect questions.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Clarify -- to make something clearer and easier to understand (esclarecer)
"Could you clarify what you mean by that?"
Confirm -- to say that something is definitely true or agreed (confirmar)
"Let me confirm the date with you."
Double-check -- to check something again to be completely sure (verificar de novo)
"I want to double-check the deadline."
Rephrase -- to say something again in a different way (reformular)
"Could you rephrase that, please?"
Agenda -- the list of topics to discuss in a meeting (pauta)
"The first item on the agenda is the rollout."
Raise -- to bring up a topic or question to discuss (levantar / trazer à tona)
"I would like to raise one point."
Address -- to deal with a question, problem, or concern (tratar / lidar com)
"Let us address the security issue first."
Concern -- a worry or doubt you want to talk about (preocupação)
"I have one concern about the timeline."
Recap -- a short summary of the main points (recapitulação / resumo)
"Let me recap the action items."
Detail -- a specific, small piece of information (detalhe)
"Could you give me more detail on that?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Clarify
Confirm
Double-check
Rephrase
Agenda
Raise
Address
Concern
Recap
Detail

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Before the call with David, Patrícia prepares her questions. She wants to sound polite, so she uses indirect questions. Instead of "What is the deadline?", she asks, "Could you tell me what the deadline is?" Instead of "Is the approval ready?", she asks, "Do you know if the approval is ready?" She also writes, "I was wondering when the call starts," and "Can I ask who owns the training?" Notice the word order: after the opener, the subject comes first, then the verb — and there is no do or does.

1. Por que a forma indireta usa "what the deadline is" e não "what is the deadline"?
A Porque a pergunta indireta sempre usa o passado.
B Porque depois do opener usamos a ordem de afirmação: sujeito + verbo.
C Porque "deadline" é um verbo irregular.
2. Para uma pergunta de sim/não ("Is the approval ready?"), a forma indireta acrescenta:
A "if" ou "whether" antes do sujeito.
B "do" antes do sujeito.
C nada; a ordem fica igual à pergunta direta.
3. Which indirect question is correct?
A "Do you know when does the call start?"
B "Do you know when the call starts?"
C "Do you know when starts the call?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Direct & Indirect Questions

GRAMMAR

Como transformar uma pergunta direta numa indireta e educada (explicação em inglês e português).

TypeStructure / EstruturaExample
Direct (Wh-)Pergunta direta: palavra-Wh + verbo + sujeito.What is the status?
Indirect (Wh-)Educada: opener + Wh + sujeito + verbo.Could you tell me what the status is?
Direct (Yes/No)Pergunta direta de sim/não: auxiliar + sujeito.Is the report ready?
Indirect (Yes/No)Use if/whether: opener + if + sujeito + verbo.Do you know if the report is ready?
Openers & do/doesOpeners: Could you tell me... · Do you know... · I was wondering... · Can I ask... · Sem do/does depois: "when does it start" → "when it starts".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Could you tell me what the deadline ?"
"Do you know the report is ready?"
"I was wondering when the call ."
"Can I ask who this task?"
"Could you what you mean?"

Stage 2: Ask a Question in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de fazer uma pergunta clara numa call na ordem correta.

?If you did not understand, ask them to rephrase it.
?Listen and wait for the right moment to ask.
?At the end, confirm and recap the action items.
?Open politely: "Sorry, can I ask...?"
?Ask your indirect question in statement order.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Could you tell me what the status is?
Você poderia me dizer qual é o status?
Do you know if the approval is ready?
Você sabe se a aprovação está pronta?
I was wondering when the call starts.
Eu queria saber quando a call começa.
Sorry, could you rephrase that, please?
Desculpe, você poderia reformular isso, por favor?
Let me recap the action items.
Deixa eu recapitular os itens de ação.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

You want to ask for the deadline politely. You say:
A "Could you tell me what the deadline is?"
B "Could you tell me what is the deadline?"
C "Could you tell me what the deadline does?"
You are not sure the report is ready. You ask:
A "Do you know is the report ready?"
B "Do you know if the report is ready?"
C "Do you know if is the report ready?"
You did not understand what David said. You ask him to:
A "Sorry, could you rephrase that, please?"
B "Sorry, could you raise that, please?"
C "Sorry, could you agenda that, please?"
At the end of the call, you want a short summary. You say:
A "Could you clarify the main points?"
B "Could you recap the main points?"
C "Could you concern the main points?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Think of a real call you have soon. Ask three indirect questions you need to ask your team: one with "Could you tell me what...", one with "Do you know if...", and one with "I was wondering when...". Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1Could you tell me what the status is?Você poderia me dizer qual é o status?
2Do you know if the approval is ready?Você sabe se a aprovação está pronta?
3Sorry, could you rephrase that, please?Desculpe, você poderia reformular isso?
4I have one concern about the timeline.Tenho uma preocupação sobre o cronograma.
5Let me recap the action items.Deixa eu recapitular os itens de ação.
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

Review -- A Customer Check-in Call

Putting Block 1 together in one real check-in call. Review words: account, stakeholder, onboarding, experience, handle, update, on track, blocker, follow up, agenda, clarify, recap. Structures: present continuous (now) · present simple (role) · present perfect vs past simple (experience) · indirect questions.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Revisão do Bloco 1. Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Account -- a company or customer you take care of (conta/cliente)
"I manage thirty premium accounts."
Stakeholder -- a person with an interest in the project (parte interessada)
"Every stakeholder joins the kickoff call."
Onboarding -- helping a new customer start using the product (integração inicial)
"We are onboarding the client this week."
Experience -- knowledge you gain from doing a job over time (experiência)
"I have ten years of experience."
Handle -- to deal with or manage something (lidar com)
"I handle the difficult escalations."
Update -- the latest news about a project's status (atualização)
"Let me give you a quick update."
On track -- going as planned, on schedule (no rumo certo)
"The rollout is on track for Friday."
Blocker -- something that stops the work from moving forward (impedimento)
"There is one blocker to solve."
Follow up -- to check or act on something after a meeting (dar seguimento)
"I will follow up with you tomorrow."
Agenda -- the list of topics for a meeting (pauta)
"Let me share the agenda for our call."
Clarify -- to make something clearer (esclarecer)
"Could you clarify the second point?"
Recap -- a short summary of the main points (recapitulação)
"Let me give you a quick recap."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Account
Stakeholder
Onboarding
Experience
Handle
Update
On track
Blocker
Follow up
Agenda
Clarify
Recap

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Patrícia is preparing for a check-in call with Mark. She manages the Acme account, and she has worked in customer success for ten years. Right now, her team is onboarding Mark's users. Last week, they finished the data migration, and she has prepared the training sessions. On the call she will ask: "Could you tell me if you need extra licenses?" The next release goes live on Friday.

1. Por que "her team is onboarding Mark's users" usa o present continuous?
A Porque é uma rotina semanal.
B Porque a ação está acontecendo agora.
C Porque terminou num dia específico.
2. "She has worked in customer success for ten years" usa o present perfect porque:
A É experiência que começou no passado e continua até hoje (for ten years).
B É uma ação que acontece neste momento.
C É uma rotina de toda semana.
3. Which indirect question has the correct word order?
A "Could you tell me if do you need extra licenses?"
B "Could you tell me if you need extra licenses?"
C "Could you tell me if need you extra licenses?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Four Structures of Block 1

GRAMMAR

As quatro estruturas que você usa numa call (explicação em inglês e português).

StructureUse / UsoExample
Present Continuous
am / is / are + -ing		Ação acontecendo agora. Happening now.We are onboarding your team.
Present Simple
verb / verb-s		Seu papel e rotinas. Your role and routines.I manage thirty accounts.
Present Perfect
have / has + particípio		Experiência, sem hora exata; com since/for. Experience, no exact time.I have worked here for ten years.
Past Simple
verb-ed		Ação terminada com hora exata. Finished action, exact time.I joined in 2019.
Indirect Question
Could you tell me + sujeito + verbo		Pergunta educada; ordem normal (sem inversão). Polite question, normal order.Could you tell me when it is ready?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Right now, we your team."
"I thirty premium accounts."
"I here for ten years."
"I the team in 2019."
"Could you tell me when the release ready?"

Stage 2: Run the Check-in in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma call de check-in na ordem correta.

?Ask the client one question (indirect question).
?Open the call and share the agenda.
?Recap and agree on the next follow-up.
?Give the status of what is happening right now.
?Say what you have finished and what is pending.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Right now, we are onboarding your team.
Agora, estamos integrando o time de vocês.
I have worked in customer success for ten years.
Eu trabalho com customer success há dez anos.
We finished the migration last week.
Nós terminamos a migração na semana passada.
Could you tell me when the next release is ready?
Você poderia me dizer quando o próximo release fica pronto?
Let me give you a quick recap of the call.
Deixe-me fazer uma recapitulação rápida da call.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

Mark asks: "What are you working on right now?" You answer:
A "Right now, we are onboarding your team."
B "Right now, we onboard your team."
C "Right now, we have onboard your team."
You describe your experience. Which is correct?
A "I work in customer success since 2015."
B "I have worked in customer success since 2015."
C "I am working in customer success since 2015."
You want to ask the client politely. The best question is:
A "Could you tell me if the data is ready?"
B "Could you tell me if is the data ready?"
C "Could you tell me if does the data ready?"
You mention a finished task with an exact time. You say:
A "We finished the migration last week."
B "We have finished the migration last week."
C "We are finishing the migration last week."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are running a check-in call with a real client. Open the call, say what is happening right now, what you have finished, what is pending, and ask one polite question to close. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1Let me share the agenda for our call.Deixe-me compartilhar a pauta da nossa call.
2Right now, we are onboarding your team.Agora, estamos integrando o time de vocês.
3I have worked on this account for two years.Eu trabalho nesta conta há dois anos.
4Could you tell me when the data is ready?Você poderia me dizer quando os dados ficam prontos?
5Let me give you a quick recap before we finish.Deixe-me fazer uma recapitulação rápida antes de terminarmos.
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

Onboarding & Driving Adoption

Guiding a new customer from day one to real value. Key words: enable, walkthrough, configure, license, champion, training, best practice, value, goal, success plan. Structure: sequencing words (first, then, after that, once, finally).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Enable -- to give someone the ability or power to do something (capacitar / habilitar)
"Our goal is to enable your team to work on their own."
Walkthrough -- a step-by-step demonstration that shows how something works (demonstração guiada)
"We book a hands-on walkthrough for your users."
Configure -- to set up the settings and options so a tool works for you (configurar)
"We help you configure the platform on day one."
License -- a paid right that lets a person use the software (licença)
"We set up your first license together."
Champion -- the person on the customer side who promotes and supports the tool (defensor interno)
"We confirm who your internal champion will be."
Training -- sessions that teach users how to use the product well (treinamento)
"We move on to training once your users feel ready."
Best practice -- the most effective, proven way of doing something (boa prática)
"We share the best practices our strongest teams use."
Value -- the real benefit or worth a customer gets from the product (valor / benefício)
"Adoption grows when people see the value for themselves."
Goal -- the result a customer plans and wants to reach (objetivo / meta)
"First, we agree on your main goals."
Success plan -- a written plan of a customer's goals and the steps to reach them (plano de sucesso)
"We review your success plan every month."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Enable
Walkthrough
Configure
License
Champion
Training
Best practice
Value
Goal
Success plan

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

When a new customer signs up, Patrícia follows a clear order. First, she runs a kickoff call and agrees on their goals. Then, her team helps them configure the platform and set up the first license. After that, she books a hands-on walkthrough for the users. Once the users feel comfortable, she moves on to training and shares the best practices. Finally, when the team is fully onboarded, she reviews the success plan and checks the progress on adoption.

1. Por que usamos "After that" e não apenas "After" para ligar o próximo passo?
A Porque "after" só existe no passado.
B Porque "after" sozinho não liga dois passos; o correto é "after that".
C Porque "after that" significa "finalmente".
2. Depois de "once", qual forma do verbo usamos?
A O presente: "once the users feel comfortable" (nunca "will").
B Sempre "will": "once the users will feel".
C Sempre o passado: "once the users felt".
3. Which sentence is correct?
A "After, we book a walkthrough."
B "After that, we book a walkthrough."
C "After that, we will booking a walkthrough."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Sequencing Words

GRAMMAR

Como colocar os passos de um processo em ordem (explicação em inglês e português).

WordUse / UsoExample
FirstO primeiro passo. Vem com vírgula no início.First, we agree on your goals.
Then / After thatO próximo passo. Use "after that" (não "after" sozinho).Then, we configure it. After that, we train.
Once"assim que" / "depois que" + presente (nunca "will").Once your users feel ready, we start.
FinallyO último passo.Finally, we celebrate the wins.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

", we agree on the customer's goals."
"Then, we configure the platform. After , we add the users."
"Once the team ready, we start training."
", when everyone is onboarded, we celebrate the wins."
"We the platform together on day one."

Stage 2: Put the Onboarding in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de um onboarding na ordem correta.

?After that, run a hands-on walkthrough for the users.
?First, run a kickoff call and agree on the goals.
?Once the users feel comfortable, move on to training.
?Confirm who the internal champion will be.
?Then, configure the platform and set up the first license.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

First, we agree on your goals.
Primeiro, combinamos os seus objetivos.
Then, we configure the platform.
Depois, configuramos a plataforma.
After that, we book a walkthrough.
Em seguida, agendamos uma demonstração guiada.
Once your users feel ready, we start training.
Assim que seus usuários se sentirem prontos, começamos o treinamento.
Adoption grows when people see the value.
A adoção cresce quando as pessoas veem o valor.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A new customer joins. You explain the very first step. You say:
A "First, we agree on your goals."
B "Finally, we agree on your goals."
C "After that, we agree on your goals."
You want to link the next step after configuring. You say:
A "After, we add the users."
B "After that, we add the users."
C "After that, we will adding the users."
You explain when training starts. You say:
A "Once your team will feel ready, we start training."
B "Once your team feels ready, we start training."
C "Once your team feel ready, we will started training."
A customer is not using the tool. You want them to get the benefit. You talk about:
A "the license number."
B "driving adoption so they see the value."
C "the agenda of the call."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Think of a real customer you onboard. Explain your plan in order: use "first", "then", "after that", "once" and "finally". Say who the champion is and how you will drive adoption. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1First, we agree on your goals.Primeiro, combinamos os seus objetivos.
2Then, we configure the platform.Depois, configuramos a plataforma.
3After that, we book a walkthrough.Em seguida, agendamos uma demonstração guiada.
4Once your users feel ready, we start training.Assim que seus usuários se sentirem prontos, começamos o treinamento.
5Adoption grows when people see the value.A adoção cresce quando as pessoas veem o valor.
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

Understanding Customer Needs

Listening for a customer's needs and recommending the right solution. Key words: need, requirement, expectation, fit, solution, recommend, suggest, option, benefit, outcome. Structure: modals of suggestion & recommendation (should / could / I'd recommend / why don't you).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Need -- something a customer must have to succeed (necessidade)
"Their main need is faster onboarding."
Requirement -- a specific thing that is necessary or demanded (requisito / exigência)
"Clear reporting is a key requirement."
Expectation -- what a customer believes will or should happen (expectativa)
"The solution should meet their expectation."
Fit -- how well a solution matches a need (encaixe / adequação)
"Let us find the right fit for your team."
Solution -- a product or way to solve a problem (solução)
"This solution fits your main need."
Recommend -- to say that something is good or right to do (recomendar)
"I would recommend the premium option."
Suggest -- to offer an idea for someone to consider (sugerir)
"Can I suggest a quick demo?"
Option -- one of several things you can choose (opção)
"You have two options for the rollout."
Benefit -- a good result or advantage (benefício)
"The main benefit is less manual work."
Outcome -- the final result of an action or process (resultado / desfecho)
"A happy customer is the best outcome."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Need
Requirement
Expectation
Fit
Solution
Recommend
Suggest
Option
Benefit
Outcome

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

On a discovery call, Patrícia listens for the customer's main need. Mike says onboarding is slow, so she makes a recommendation. She says, "You should enable the new dashboard," and "You could start with a small rollout." For a softer idea, she adds, "Why don't you book a quick demo?" When she gives strong advice, she says, "I'd recommend training the team first," or "I recommend that you train the team first." Notice the form: after should, could, and why don't you, the verb has no to; after recommend, she uses -ing or that you + base verb — never "recommend you to".

1. Por que dizemos "You should enable" e não "You should to enable"?
A Porque "enable" é um verbo irregular.
B Porque depois de should/could usamos o verbo na forma base, sem "to".
C Porque "should" sempre pede o passado.
2. Depois de "recommend", qual forma do verbo usamos?
A "-ing" (recommend training) ou "(that) you + base" (recommend that you train).
B "to + verbo" (recommend you to train).
C sempre o passado (recommend trained).
3. Which recommendation is correct?
A "Why don't you to book a demo?"
B "Why don't you book a demo?"
C "Why don't you booking a demo?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Recommending (should / could / recommend)

GRAMMAR

Como recomendar uma solução de forma clara e educada (explicação em inglês e português).

FormStructure / EstruturaExample
should / couldConselho: should/could + verbo base (sem "to").You should enable it.
I'd recommendRecomendação: I'd recommend + verbo-ing.I'd recommend training the team.
I recommend thatAlternativa: I recommend (that) you + verbo base.I recommend that you train the team.
Why don't youSugestão leve: Why don't you + verbo base?Why don't you book a demo?
CuidadoNunca "should TO" nem "recommend you TO". Depois de should/could: verbo base. Depois de recommend: -ing ou "that you + base".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"You should the dashboard."
"I'd starting with onboarding."
"Why don't you a quick demo?"
"You could the premium option."
"I recommend that you the team first."

Stage 2: Run a Discovery Call in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de um discovery call na ordem correta.

?Recommend the solution that fits their need.
?Ask about the customer's main need.
?Name the benefit and agree on the outcome.
?Listen and confirm any specific requirement.
?Suggest one or two options to consider.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

What is your team's main need right now?
Qual é a principal necessidade da sua equipe agora?
I would recommend starting with a guided rollout.
Eu recomendaria começar com um rollout guiado.
You should enable the analytics dashboard.
Você deveria ativar o painel de análises.
Why don't you book a quick demo?
Por que você não agenda uma demo rápida?
That solution fits your main need.
Essa solução atende à sua principal necessidade.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

You want to give strong advice to enable the dashboard. You say:
A "You should enable the dashboard."
B "You should to enable the dashboard."
C "You should enabling the dashboard."
You recommend training the team. You say:
A "I recommend you to train the team."
B "I recommend that you train the team."
C "I recommend you training to the team."
You want to offer a soft idea to try a demo. You say:
A "Why don't you book a quick demo?"
B "Why don't you to book a quick demo?"
C "Why you don't book a quick demo?"
A customer asks what good result the plan gives. You talk about the:
A "requirement of the plan."
B "benefit of the plan."
C "expectation of the plan."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Think of a real customer you support. Describe their main need, then make three recommendations: one with "You should...", one with "I'd recommend...", and one with "Why don't you...?". Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1What is your team's main need right now?Qual é a principal necessidade da sua equipe agora?
2I'd recommend starting with a guided rollout.Eu recomendaria começar com um rollout guiado.
3You should enable the analytics dashboard.Você deveria ativar o painel de análises.
4Why don't you book a quick demo?Por que você não agenda uma demo rápida?
5That solution fits your main need.Essa solução atende à sua principal necessidade.
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

Handling a Technical Issue

Report a technical issue and its result like a customer success manager. New words: issue, bug, ticket, root cause, workaround, resolve, escalate, log, reproduce, fix. Grammar: present perfect for results ("we have fixed", "it has resolved").
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Issue -- a problem that stops something from working (problema/falha)
"We have identified the issue."
Bug -- an error in the software that makes it behave wrongly (erro de software)
"The team has fixed the bug."
Ticket -- a record of a customer problem in the support system (chamado)
"I have opened a ticket for you."
Root cause -- the real, underlying reason a problem happens (causa raiz)
"We have found the root cause."
Workaround -- a temporary way to keep working around a problem (solução temporária)
"We have shared a quick workaround."
Resolve -- to solve a problem completely so it does not return (resolver)
"Support has resolved the problem."
Escalate -- to pass a problem to a higher or more expert team (escalar/encaminhar)
"I have escalated this to our engineers."
Log -- a record of what the system has done, used to find errors (registro/log)
"We have checked the server logs."
Reproduce -- to make the same problem happen again, to study it (reproduzir)
"We have reproduced the error."
Fix -- a permanent solution that repairs the problem for good (correção/conserto)
"We have deployed a fix."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Issue
Bug
Ticket
Root cause
Workaround
Resolve
Escalate
Log
Reproduce
Fix

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

A customer opened a ticket: users could not log in. Patrícia has escalated the issue to the engineers. So far, the team has reproduced the bug and has checked the logs. They have found the root cause — a recent update changed one setting. This morning they have applied a fix, and support has resolved the problem. They tested it twice an hour ago, and the issue has not come back.

1. Por que "the team has reproduced the bug" usa o present perfect?
A Porque aconteceu num dia específico no passado.
B Porque é um resultado que importa agora (já foi feito).
C Porque é uma rotina de toda semana.
2. "They tested it twice an hour ago" usa o past simple porque:
A Há um tempo passado exato e fechado ("an hour ago").
B É um resultado sem hora definida.
C É uma ação acontecendo neste momento.
3. Which sentence reports a result correctly?
A "Support has resolved the problem yesterday."
B "Support has resolved the problem."
C "Support has resolve the problem."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect for Results

GRAMMAR

Como reportar um resultado que importa agora (explicação em inglês e português).

Form / FormaUse / UsoExample
Affirmative
have / has + particípio		Resultado que importa agora; sem hora exata. A result that matters now.We have fixed the bug.
Negative
have / has not + particípio		O problema não voltou. Something has not happened.The issue has not come back.
Question
Have / Has + sujeito + particípio		Perguntar sobre um resultado; com yet/already. Asking about a result.Have you checked the logs yet?
Past Simple
verbo-ed (contraste)		Hora passada exata e fechada. Finished past time (yesterday, an hour ago).We tested it an hour ago.

Use o present perfect com just, already, yet, so far (sem hora exata). Depois de have/has vem o particípio: fixed, resolved, found, run (NUNCA "have ran" nem "has fix").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"So far, we the root cause."
"The team the issue."
"We have the bug on a test account."
"We a fix this morning."
"Have you the logs yet?"

Stage 2: Handle the Issue in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de como lidar com um problema técnico na ordem correta.

?Apply a fix and test it.
?Open a ticket for the issue.
?Tell the customer it has been resolved.
?Reproduce the bug and check the logs.
?Find the root cause.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

We have resolved the issue.
Nós resolvemos o problema.
We have found the root cause.
Nós encontramos a causa raiz.
I have escalated this to our engineers.
Eu escalei isto para os nossos engenheiros.
We have shared a quick workaround.
Nós compartilhamos uma solução temporária rápida.
Have you checked the logs yet?
Você já verificou os logs?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

The customer asks for a status. The bug is solved now. You say:
A "Good news — we have fixed the bug."
B "Good news — we have fix the bug."
C "Good news — we fixing the bug."
You give the result with an exact past time. Which is correct?
A "We have resolved it an hour ago."
B "We resolved it an hour ago."
C "We are resolving it an hour ago."
The issue is still open. You give an honest status:
A "We have reproduced the bug and escalated it. A fix is coming."
B "We have reproduce the bug and escalate it."
C "We reproducing the bug since this morning."
You want to ask the engineer about progress. The best question is:
A "Have you found the root cause yet?"
B "Have you found the root cause already yesterday?"
C "Did you have found the root cause?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a technical issue happened at your company. Give a short status update to the customer: name the problem, the root cause, what your team has already done (present perfect), and the fix. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1We have identified the issue.Nós identificamos o problema.
2We have found the root cause.Nós encontramos a causa raiz.
3I have escalated this to our engineers.Eu escalei isto para os nossos engenheiros.
4Good news, we have resolved the issue.Boa notícia, nós resolvemos o problema.
5Have you checked the logs yet?Você já verificou os logs?
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Escalations Under Pressure

Handling a critical escalation on a customer call. Key words: escalation, critical, urgent, impact, severity, downtime, deadline, commitment, unless, as soon as. Structure: first conditional + urgency (If it fails, we will escalate).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Escalation -- moving a problem to a higher, more urgent level of support (escalação)
"This needs an escalation to the engineering team."
Critical -- extremely serious; it needs action right now (crítico)
"The outage is critical, so we cannot wait."
Urgent -- needs a fast response, very soon (urgente)
"This is urgent — please reply as soon as you can."
Impact -- the effect a problem has on the customer's work (impacto)
"The impact on the sales team is huge."
Severity -- how serious a problem is (gravidade, severidade)
"We raised the severity to level one."
Downtime -- time when a system is not working (tempo fora do ar, indisponibilidade)
"Every minute of downtime costs the client money."
Deadline -- the time by which something must be done (prazo)
"We have a hard deadline tomorrow morning."
Commitment -- a promise to do something (compromisso)
"I made a commitment to update him every thirty minutes."
Unless -- except if; if not (a menos que, a não ser que)
"Unless we act now, we will lose the account."
As soon as -- at the moment that; immediately when (assim que)
"As soon as I have news, I will call you."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Escalation
Critical
Urgent
Impact
Severity
Downtime
Deadline
Commitment
Unless
As soon as

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

David's dashboard is down, and the impact on his team is huge. The problem is critical, so Patrícia raises an escalation. "If it is a server problem, our engineers will start immediately," she says. She makes a clear commitment: "As soon as I have an update, I will call you." She also warns him: "Unless we act fast, we will miss the deadline." The team raises the severity to level one. If the first fix does not work, they will roll back and protect his data.

1. Por que "If it is a server problem" usa o presente, e não "will"?
A Porque a ação já terminou no passado.
B Porque a parte da condição (if) fica no presente; só o resultado usa will.
C Porque é uma rotina que se repete todo dia.
2. "Unless we act fast" tem o mesmo sentido de:
A "If we do not act fast" (se não agirmos rápido).
B "If we act fast" (se agirmos rápido).
C "When we always act fast" (quando sempre agimos rápido).
3. Which sentence is correct?
A "As soon as I will have news, I will call you."
B "As soon as I have news, I will call you."
C "As soon as I have news, I call you next month."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- First Conditional & Urgency

GRAMMAR

Como falar de um resultado futuro que depende de uma condição (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
First Conditional
If + present, ... will + verb		Resultado futuro real que depende de uma condição. A real future result.If it fails, we will escalate.
as soon as / whenA parte do tempo fica no presente — nunca "will". The time clause stays present.As soon as I have news, I will call.
unless = if notJá é negativo. Não use outro "not". Already negative, no double "not".Unless we act = If we do not act.
Common errorsNão use will depois de if / as soon as / unless. O resultado SEMPRE usa will.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"If the system goes down, we escalate."
"As soon as I an update, I will call you."
"Unless we now, we will miss the deadline."
"If the fix does not work, we roll back."
"This is , so we cannot wait."
"I made a clear to update him every thirty minutes."

Stage 2: Handle the Escalation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de conduzir uma escalação crítica na ordem correta.

?Make a commitment: "As soon as I have news, I will call you."
?Recognize the impact and how critical the problem is.
?Explain the plan with first conditional (if the fix works / unless...).
?Set the severity level (raise it to critical / S1).
?Raise the escalation to the engineering team.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

I am escalating this to critical severity right now.
Estou escalando isto para severidade crítica agora mesmo.
As soon as I have an update, I will call you.
Assim que eu tiver uma atualização, eu te ligo.
If the system goes down, we will escalate immediately.
Se o sistema cair, nós escalamos imediatamente.
Unless we act now, we will lose the account.
A menos que a gente aja agora, vamos perder a conta.
I made a clear commitment to update you every thirty minutes.
Eu fiz um compromisso claro de te atualizar a cada trinta minutos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

The customer's system is down. You want to start the escalation. You say:
A "If it will be a server problem, we escalate."
B "If it is a server problem, we will escalate immediately."
C "If it is a server problem, we escalated immediately."
You want to promise an update without waiting. The best answer is:
A "As soon as I have news, I will call you."
B "As soon as I will have news, I call you."
C "As soon as I had news, I will call you."
You warn the customer about the deadline. Which is correct?
A "Unless we don't act now, we will miss the deadline."
B "Unless we act now, we will miss the deadline."
C "Unless we acting now, we will miss the deadline."
The customer asks how serious it is. You answer:
A "It is critical. I am raising the severity to level one."
B "It is critical. I raise the downtime to level one."
C "It is a deadline. I am raising the impact to level one."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

A critical outage just started on your biggest account. Take the call: recognize the impact, set the severity, escalate, and make a clear commitment using first conditional (if / unless / as soon as). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1I am escalating this to critical severity right now.Estou escalando isto para severidade crítica agora mesmo.
2As soon as I have an update, I will call you.Assim que eu tiver uma atualização, eu te ligo.
3If the fix does not work, we will roll back.Se a correção não funcionar, vamos reverter.
4Unless we act now, we will lose the account.A menos que a gente aja agora, vamos perder a conta.
5I made a clear commitment to update you every thirty minutes.Eu fiz um compromisso claro de te atualizar a cada trinta minutos.
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Review -- Resolving a Critical Escalation

Block 2 review through a critical escalation case study. Review words: escalation, critical, severity, impact, downtime, root cause, workaround, resolve, commitment, deadline. Grammar review: sequencing, suggestion modals, present perfect for results, and the first conditional.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Revise cada palavra do Block 2 e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Escalation -- moving a problem to a higher, more urgent level of support (escalação)
"This needs a real escalation tonight."
Critical -- extremely serious; it needs action right now (crítico)
"This is a critical issue for the customer."
Severity -- how serious a problem is, on a scale (gravidade, severidade)
"The customer raised the severity to level one."
Impact -- the effect a problem has on the customer's work (impacto)
"The impact on the business was high."
Downtime -- time when a system is not working (tempo fora do ar)
"The downtime lasted almost three hours."
Root cause -- the real, underlying reason a problem happens (causa raiz)
"We confirmed the root cause in the logs."
Workaround -- a temporary way to keep working around a problem (solução temporária)
"We shared a quick workaround during the outage."
Resolve -- to solve a problem completely so it does not return (resolver)
"By the evening, the team has resolved the issue."
Commitment -- a promise to do something (compromisso)
"She made a firm commitment to the client."
Deadline -- the time by which something must be done (prazo)
"The team met the customer's deadline."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Escalation
Severity
Downtime
Root cause
Workaround
Resolve
Commitment
Deadline

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

A customer opened a critical ticket and raised the severity to level one because the impact was high. First, the team confirmed the root cause. Then they shared a workaround during the downtime. Patrícia made a clear commitment: "If the problem comes back, we will call you." By the evening, the team has resolved the issue and met the deadline.

1. Por que "the team has resolved the issue" usa o present perfect?
A Porque aconteceu num dia específico no passado.
B Porque é um resultado que importa agora (já foi feito).
C Porque é uma rotina de toda semana.
2. "If the problem comes back, we will call you" é um first conditional porque:
A Usa if + presente ("comes") e will + verbo ("will call").
B Usa will nas duas partes da frase.
C Fala de algo que já aconteceu.
3. Which sentence opens the steps in the right order?
A "Then we confirmed the cause, first we shared a workaround."
B "First we confirmed the cause, then we shared a workaround."
C "First we shared a workaround, first we confirmed the cause."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Block 2 Toolkit

GRAMMAR

As quatro ferramentas gramaticais do Block 2, juntas (explicação em inglês e português).

Tool / FerramentaUse / UsoExample
Sequencing
first / then / after that		Colocar os passos em ordem. Put the steps in order.First we found the cause, then we fixed it.
Suggestion modals
should / could / I'd recommend		Recomendar uma solução com educação. Recommend politely.I would recommend a workaround.
Present perfect
have / has + particípio		Reportar um resultado que importa agora. Report a result now.We have resolved the issue.
First conditional
if + presente, will + verbo		Condição real futura + resultado. Real future condition.If it comes back, we will call you.

No first conditional, a parte do if fica no presente (NUNCA "if it will come back"). Depois de have/has vem o particípio: resolved, found, shared.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" we confirm the root cause, then we share a workaround."
"I a workaround for now."
"So far, the team the issue."
"If the platform down again, we will call you."
"We raised the to level one."

Stage 2: Handle the Escalation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma escalação crítica na ordem correta.

?Make a commitment: an update every thirty minutes.
?Raise the severity and start the escalation.
?Resolve the issue and meet the deadline.
?Confirm the root cause in the logs.
?Share a quick workaround during the downtime.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

I have raised the severity to level one.
Eu elevei a severidade para o nível um.
I would recommend a quick workaround.
Eu recomendaria uma solução temporária rápida.
We have resolved the issue.
Nós resolvemos o problema.
If the problem comes back, we will call you.
Se o problema voltar, nós ligaremos para você.
Here is my commitment to you.
Aqui está o meu compromisso com você.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A critical outage just started. You open the escalation. You say:
A "I have raised the severity to level one and started a full escalation."
B "I have raise the severity and started a escalation."
C "I raising the severity since now."
You make a commitment for tonight. Which first conditional is correct?
A "If the problem will come back, we will call you."
B "If the problem comes back, we will call you."
C "If the problem comes back, we call you back already."
You report the result with an exact past time. Which is correct?
A "We have resolved it an hour ago."
B "We resolved it an hour ago."
C "We are resolving it an hour ago."
The customer wants help during the outage. You suggest:
A "I would recommend a quick workaround so your team can keep working."
B "I will recommend you a workaround tomorrow maybe."
C "You must to use a workaround now."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a critical escalation at your company. Give a short update to the customer: name the severity and impact, sequence what your team has done (present perfect), and make a commitment with the first conditional ("if it comes back, we will..."). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1I have raised the severity to level one.Eu elevei a severidade para o nível um.
2I would recommend a quick workaround.Eu recomendaria uma solução temporária rápida.
3We have found the root cause.Nós encontramos a causa raiz.
4If the problem comes back, we will call you.Se o problema voltar, nós ligaremos para você.
5Here is my commitment to you.Aqui está o meu compromisso com você.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Working Across Cultures (India/US)

Communicating clearly and politely with global teams. Key words: culture, directness, etiquette, norm, adapt, context, time zone, overlap, rapport, misunderstanding. Structure: polite directness — softening requests (Could you possibly...? / Would it be okay if...? / Would you mind...?).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Culture -- the shared values, habits, and ways of working of a group (cultura)
"Every team has its own work culture."
Directness -- how openly and plainly people say what they mean (franqueza / objetividade)
"Directness feels normal in the US, but strong elsewhere."
Etiquette -- the polite rules for how to behave in a group (etiqueta)
"Email etiquette changes from country to country."
Norm -- a usual, accepted way of doing something (norma / padrão)
"Quick replies are the norm on this team."
Adapt -- to change your style to fit a new situation (adaptar)
"I adapt my tone for each global team."
Context -- the full situation around what is said or done (contexto)
"The same words mean different things in another context."
Time zone -- a region that shares the same official time (fuso horário)
"India and the US are in very different time zones."
Overlap -- the shared hours when two teams are both online (sobreposição de horários)
"We only have a two-hour overlap each day."
Rapport -- a friendly, trusting connection with someone (sintonia / boa relação)
"A few minutes of small talk builds rapport."
Misunderstanding -- a failure to understand something correctly (mal-entendido)
"A short message avoided a big misunderstanding."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Culture
Directness
Etiquette
Norm
Adapt
Context
Time zone
Overlap
Rapport
Misunderstanding

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Patrícia works with teams in the US and India. She learns that the same request can sound polite in one culture and too direct in another, so she adapts. Instead of "Send the deck now," she writes, "Could you possibly send the deck?" Instead of "Move the call," she asks, "Would it be okay if we moved the call?" When there is no rush, she says, "When you get a chance, could you review the doc?" And she often uses "Would you mind sending the numbers?" Notice the patterns: could you possibly + base verb, would you mind + -ing, and would it be okay if + a past verb.

1. Por que a Patrícia troca "Send the deck now" por "Could you possibly send the deck?"
A Porque a ordem direta está gramaticalmente errada.
B Porque o opener educado suaviza o pedido sem perder a clareza.
C Porque "deck" precisa de um verbo no passado.
2. Depois de "Would you mind", qual forma do verbo usamos?
A O verbo com -ing: "Would you mind sending...?"
B O verbo no infinitivo com to: "Would you mind to send...?"
C O verbo no passado: "Would you mind sent...?"
3. Which request is polite and correct?
A "Would it be okay if we move the call?"
B "Would it be okay if we moved the call?"
C "Would it be okay if moving the call?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Polite Directness (Softening Requests)

GRAMMAR

Como transformar uma ordem direta num pedido educado (explicação em inglês e português).

OpenerStructure / EstruturaExample
Could you possibly+ verbo na base. Pedido educado e claro.Could you possibly send the deck?
Would you mind+ verbo com -ing.Would you mind sending the deck?
Would it be okay if+ sujeito + verbo no passado.Would it be okay if we moved the call?
When you get a chance,+ um pedido normal. Tira a pressão.When you get a chance, could you review it?
Por quê?Uma ordem seca ("Send it now") pode soar rude em algumas culturas. O opener educado mantém o pedido claro, mas adiciona respeito — isso é polite directness.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Could you share the deck before the call?"
"Would it be if we moved the sync to 8 a.m.?"
"Would you sending me the final numbers?"
"When you get a , could you review the doc?"
"A short message helped us avoid a big ."

Stage 2: Open a Global Call in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de abrir uma call com um time global na ordem correta.

?Make your request politely: "Could you possibly...?"
?Build a little rapport with friendly small talk.
?Check one detail to avoid a misunderstanding.
?Notice the team's norms and adapt your directness.
?Protect the overlap: agree on a good time to talk.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Could you possibly share the deck before the call?
Você poderia, por favor, compartilhar o material antes da call?
Would it be okay if we moved the sync to 8 a.m.?
Tudo bem se a gente passasse o sync para as 8h?
Would you mind sending me the final numbers?
Você se importaria de me enviar os números finais?
A few minutes of small talk builds rapport.
Alguns minutos de conversa leve criam sintonia.
Let me check one detail to avoid a misunderstanding.
Deixa eu confirmar um detalhe para evitar um mal-entendido.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

You need the deck before the call. You say:
A "Could you possibly share the deck before the call?"
B "Send me the deck before the call."
C "You must share the deck now."
You want to move the call one hour. You ask:
A "Would it be okay if we move the call?"
B "Would it be okay if we moved the call?"
C "Would it be okay if we moving the call?"
There is no rush for the review. You say:
A "When you get a chance, could you review the doc?"
B "Review the doc immediately."
C "You have to review the doc right now."
A message felt unclear. To prevent a problem, you:
A say nothing and hope it is fine.
B ask a short question to avoid a misunderstanding.
C wait a week and then complain.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Think of a real call with your India or US team. Record yourself opening it: one line to build rapport, and two polite requests — one with "Could you possibly..." and one with "Would it be okay if...". Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1Could you possibly share the deck before the call?Você poderia compartilhar o material antes da call?
2Would it be okay if we moved the sync to 8 a.m.?Tudo bem se a gente passasse o sync para as 8h?
3Would you mind sending me the final numbers?Você se importaria de me enviar os números finais?
4A few minutes of small talk builds rapport.Alguns minutos de conversa leve criam sintonia.
5Let me check one detail to avoid a misunderstanding.Deixa eu confirmar um detalhe para evitar um mal-entendido.
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Product Calls & Roadmap

Reading a product roadmap and talking about what comes next. Key words: feature, roadmap, release, enhancement, request, launch, deliver, backlog, prioritize, estimate. Structure: future forms (will / going to / present continuous for plans).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Feature -- a specific function or tool inside a product (recurso / funcionalidade)
"Each feature has a clear scope."
Roadmap -- a plan that shows what will be built and when (planejamento / mapa do produto)
"This roadmap shows what we are going to build."
Release -- a new version of the product you make available (versão / lançamento)
"This release includes two enhancements."
Enhancement -- an improvement to a feature that already exists (melhoria)
"Two enhancements that customers requested."
Request -- something a customer asks the team to build or change (pedido / solicitação)
"We deliver the features you have requested."
Launch -- to make a product or feature available to users (lançar)
"We are launching the mobile app on Tuesday."
Deliver -- to finish and hand over what you promised (entregar)
"Our goal is to deliver the features you asked for."
Backlog -- the list of requests and work still waiting to be done (fila de pendências)
"The rest stay on our backlog for a future release."
Prioritize -- to decide what to do first, by importance (priorizar)
"We prioritize the requests with the biggest impact."
Estimate -- a rough guess of how long or how much something will take (estimativa)
"We are still working on the estimate."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Feature
Roadmap
Release
Enhancement
Request
Launch
Deliver
Backlog
Prioritize
Estimate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

When a customer asks what is coming, Patrícia explains the plan. In June, the team is going to release version three of the dashboard. She tells the customer the new reports will save them time. They are launching the mobile app on Tuesday the fifteenth, so the date is already fixed. One feature is still on the backlog, so Patrícia is honest: she will not promise a date yet, but the team is going to review it with the customer next quarter.

1. "They are launching the app on Tuesday the fifteenth." Por que usamos o present continuous aqui?
A Porque a ação está acontecendo agora, neste momento.
B Porque é um arranjo futuro JÁ marcado, com data fixa.
